title: Chapter Fifty-Two: The Pursuit
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

No one would readily acknowledge it. Especially one who claimed to follow the righteous path.

Hwangbo Myeong-rin sneered. Not a flicker in her expression.

"Used your martial sister as bait? You must be seeing things. Or --" her lips curled, "-- are you trying to slander me? You rootless mongrel."

"Isn't it you who lacks roots?"

Not Yeon-shin. Hyeon Won-chang had pushed past his restraining arm and stepped forward, adjusting the hero's band on his forehead with a smile like a painting.

"Your words about mongrels hardly suit an educated person. I hear your Shandong has fine Lu cuisine -- dumplings wrapped with all manner of ingredients. Perhaps you wrapped the Four Books and Three Classics with your medicine and ate the pages. Is that how the Hwangbo family teaches Confucian principles?"

"How dare you--"

"They say Shandong food is salty. Your words are truly bland. Two people witnessed your treachery, yet you deny it without shame? I have never heard of such poor character among Shandong's martial nobility."

Hwangbo Myeong-rin's footwork shifted. Energy gathered in both hands, fierce and murderous.

In that instant, Yeon-shin's hand blurred.

Crack.

Her body spun in the air. He had struck her cheek with the full refinement of the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique behind his palm.

She fell. The other families' rising talents took their stances -- eight swords gleaming in the bright sunlight.

"If your words are true, this is justified," one said. "However, understand our position."

"There is no neutrality under heaven. Without knowing the truth, we can only side with those we share friendship with."

*Martial Families pursue profit while advocating righteousness. Their way of realising chivalry is completely different from the mountain sects.*

Yeon-shin had expected this. The group's loyalty lay with Hwangbo Myeong-rin. South Zhili bordered Shandong. These families' disciples had lived under Hwangbo authority all their lives.

He looked at Lazy Flame Dragon, who was chewing a winter cherry and staring at him with blank, fascinated eyes.

Then he looked at Hwangbo Myeong-rin's second brother -- the youth he had downed with one strike on Huizhou's main road, now kneeling to support his sister.

"I heard noble families have spirit beasts," Yeon-shin said. "Cleverly raised with clan secret techniques. Animals that understand human speech and display mysterious abilities. Call your family's warriors. As many as possible. The Blood Flame Sect may send reinforcements."

The second young master's collar rustled. A small bird resembling a swallow emerged, wings fluttering. He wrote a message with a travel brush and tied it to the bird's leg.

But did not release it.

"The moment we send this, we lose our value. You would kill us then. We decide when to release it."

"Think further. It does not end with one message. Consider the distance between South Zhili and Shandong. That bird needs to fly back and forth."

"...You are right."

The bird launched skyward. Yeon-shin watched its tail vanish against the white sky.

*So that when the Hwangbo family's military force moves south, Desolate Sect can secure justification for punishment and advance north.*

Hwangbo Myeong-rin, conscious again, glared murder. The second young master could not raise his head.

They could never harmonise. Yet they had to become companions.

"Whoever brought the blood demons," Lazy Flame Dragon said, looking between both sides, "we need to join forces. They came to kill even the Dragon Phoenix Gathering's rising talents."

He stuffed another winter cherry into his mouth. His eyes, while chewing, stared at Yeon-shin with an expression that held no malice and no fear.

Only hunger.

---

Twenty days passed.

They had shaken off the Blood Flame Sect's pursuit several times. The Heaven-Spanning Net had closed from the south -- no return to Huizhou. Their only chance was to push northward, toward the Hwangbo family's military force descending from Jinan.

The entire group looked like beggars. Mountain paths far from the main road. Hunting birds for food. Sleeping in shifts -- one of the Radiant Demon Wing's three always awake, because the Hwangbo siblings could not be trusted.

Yeon-shin had grown accustomed to the taste of roasted sparrow and the sound of Won-chang's steady breathing on watch. The pain in his body had settled into something he could carry, if not ignore.

*Now it is a race against time.*

Enough days had passed. The mission was successful. From both directions -- Hwangbo from Jinan, Desolate Sect from the south -- forces would be rushing to converge.

*They will come simultaneously.*

"The family's guard unit is on its way. It is over."

Hwangbo Myeong-rin had recovered her confidence entirely. The second young master beamed. The family warriors behind them wore blank faces.

"Gu Gwa of the Chasing Truth Sect," Myeong-rin said, pleasure in her voice. "Are you ready for the family's interrogation?"

Yeon-shin did not answer.

He was about to draw his sword when the ground shook behind him.

WHOOSH! BANG!

Someone landed at his back. An enormous vibration shook the plain -- incomparable to the thunderclap bombs. He knew without turning. The energy wave was as familiar as his own heartbeat.

"Squad Leader?" Shin So-bin cried.

Ma Jin. Radiant Demon Wing's Leader. His uncle.

BANG! BANG!

Two more impacts, one on either side. The Azure Sky Squad Leader and the Annihilation Team Leader, their presence like the weight of ancient mountains settling upon the earth.

Then -- twenty more. Thirty. The reverse wind from Desolate Sect lightness techniques swept the plain like a gale. Blue-ranked masters of three Divine Sword Squads descending from the hills and spreading left and right, their energy waves overlapping into something vast and terrible.

"Lightning Flash!"

WHIS!

A long object hurtled toward his head. Yeon-shin twisted slightly and caught it. The cool steel settled into his palm like a piece of himself returning.

The Desolate Sword. He placed it at his waist beside Northern Light.

Another object flew. A white pill, fragrant. Shaolin's Clear Righteousness pill. Cheong Myeong had seen through his internal injury without a word exchanged.

RIP!

Hyeon Won-chang tore the cloth from Yeon-shin's shoulder. The golden character Hwang was revealed -- the mark of the Desolate Sect Lord's direct disciple.

Silence. Someone swallowed empty air.

Yeon-shin rolled the pill between his fingers and looked at Hwangbo Myeong-rin.

"Domineering Sword Hwangbo Jun," he said. His voice was calm, addressed to no one and to everyone.

He held down his sword.

"You may begin."

---
